rulings.Case Title: BRIAN UGALE VS. NICHOLAS ROOMEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Request For Monetary Sanctions, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Richard L. Daniels, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law and Motion calendar for Monday, Apr-07-2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFFS BRIAN UGALE AN INDIVIDUAL, STEVEN LAUER, and BRENT LANGLOIS' Motion To Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiffs' unopposed motion to compel and for sanctions is granted. The moving papers show that Plaintiffs served on defendant George Goodwin Realty (GGR) a set of form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and requests for production of documents. GGR has not responded. The motion to compel is granted and objections are waived. GGR is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, to this discovery within 20 days of entry of this order. (See CCP 2031.300(a); 2030.290(a).) GGR is ordered to produce responsive documents within 20 days of entry of this order. Plaintiffs also seek sanctions. CCP 2031.300(c) and 2030.290(c) provide that the court shall order sanctions payable to a party who successfully obtains a motion to compel unless the opposing party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. The court does not find substantial justification by GGR and orders sanctions in the amount of $2000 payable by GGR to Plaintiffs within 20 days of entry of this order. The court reduces the requested sanctions because Plaintiffs do not provide a declaration substantiating the amount of fees and the amount awarded is reasonable on a straightforward motion like this one. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL OWENS ET AL VS. RUTH WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave Of Court To Amend The Complaint
